body.title: CLOSED: rename utility closet → rack server
body.result: superclaude echo strings + fn name → rack_server (b8e959b); utility_closet_server.py docstring + HTML + log msgs → Rack Server (f6c29336 TheIgors). Imports/file paths unchanged.
body.description:
The name 'utility closet' (from D335) appears in superclaude startup messages and server script names, which is confusing at a glance. Replace with 'rack_server' (or 'adc_server') to match the rack/shelf vocabulary used everywhere else in ADC.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/.local/bin/superclaude (start_utility_closet fn + echo strings), /home/akien/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/utility_closet_server.py (internal name/docstring), cc_log_stop_hook.py (any references), igor launcher scripts that echo the name
**Design rules:** none apply — cosmetic rename, no logic change
**Scope boundary:** In scope: user-facing echo strings in superclaude, server script internal name/docstring, any other startup-path echo strings. Out of scope: renaming the actual file (utility_closet_server.py → rack_server.py) — that's a separate migration risk; renaming Python module paths or import sites
**Test plan:** no tests because: pure string rename in shell scripts and docstrings; manual smoke test: run superclaude and confirm new name appears in startup output
